title: AWK
summary: AWK processes text files line by line, splitting each line into fields. It is ideal for extracting columns from structured output, computing totals, and filtering records — all without writing a full script.
keyPoints: $1, $2, ...: fields on the current line (split by FS)
NF: number of fields on the current line
FS: field separator (default whitespace); set with -F or in BEGIN
END { }: executed after all input is read
Pattern before { }: only run action when pattern matches

exercises.prompt: In AWK, which variable refers to the second field of a line?
exercises.explanation: Fields are numbered from 1. $1 is the first field, $2 the second, $NF is the last.

exercises.prompt: Complete the AWK command to print only lines where the first field equals 'ERROR'.
exercises.explanation: $1 refers to the first whitespace-delimited field on each line.

exercises.prompt: Write an AWK command that sums the values in the third column of numbers.txt and prints the total at the end.
exercises.hint: Accumulate in a variable in the main block, print in END.
exercises.solution: awk '{ sum += $3 } END { print sum }' numbers.txt
